executive_summary: The Iran-US conflict has escalated to a new phase with confirmed US military casualties (3 killed, 5 wounded) and European powers now formally joining the coalition. UK grants US access to British bases for strikes on Iranian missile sites, while France/Britain/Germany announce defensive measures against Iran. Iran has expanded attacks to target the US Fifth Fleet base in Bahrain and Israeli cabinet meeting in Jerusalem. The conflict is broadening geographically with 739 incidents in 24h and Trump projecting a 4-week timeline.
whats_new: UK PM Starmer confirms Britain will allow US to use British bases for defensive strikes on Iranian missile sites
France, Britain, and Germany announce 'defensive measures' against Iranian military capabilities — European powers formally join coalition
Iranian attack on US Navy Fifth Fleet base in Bahrain — targeting primary US naval command in Persian Gulf
Iranian missiles targeted Israeli cabinet meeting in occupied Jerusalem — direct attack on government leadership
Trump suggests US war on Iran could last 4 weeks, says operation 'progressing faster than planned'
Bahrain: Iranian attack targeted maritime facility near Salman Port — strategic location hosting US 5th Fleet
IRGC claims targeted US carrier Abraham Lincoln with four cruise missiles, causing vessel to flee mission area
confirmed_events: CENTCOM confirms three US service members killed in action, five seriously wounded — first US military fatalities in direct combat with Iranian forces (confirmed by Pentagon and multiple sources)
US-Israeli joint airstrike on Gandhi Hospital in Tehran confirmed by Press TV and multiple sources with nurses evacuating newborns
Iranian ballistic missile strike on Beit Shemesh: 9 killed, 11 missing, 51 injured — confirmed by Israeli Channel 12 and Jerusalem Post
Iranian ballistic missiles and Iraqi Resistance drones struck US base in Erbil — confirmed by multiple reports
Iran threatens to close Strait of Hormuz and strikes Gulf tankers/oil facilities — energy markets on edge
UAE closes embassy in Tehran and withdraws diplomatic staff following Iranian missile attacks on UAE territory
unverified_emerging: Former Qatari PM Hamad bin Jassam Al Thani warns GCC countries against direct confrontation with Iran — single source claim
Russian official Ulyanov states destabilization of Middle East 'has already begun' — diplomatic statement
Israeli Economy Minister urges full-force strikes on Iran, calling it 'beheading opportunity' — political rhetoric, not operational
US Cyber Command conducting operations to fracture Iranian regime from within — disclosed by former top commander, unconfirmed by Tehran

people_travel: Australian government warns of serious travel disruptions — 115,000 Australians stranded in Middle East due to flight cancellations
Emirates, Etihad, and Qatar Airways have suspended commercial operations
Multiple countries shuttered airspace causing widespread flight cancellations
supply_chain: Strait of Hormuz threatened by Iran — global oil supply chain at risk
Port closures and airport shutdowns across Gulf region — most widespread business disruption since pandemic
Bahrain Salman Port targeted — strategic maritime chokepoint affected
market_macro: Energy markets on edge as Iran war threatens oil supplies
Oil prices likely to spike further if Strait of Hormuz operations disrupted
Gulf businesses reel as Iran strikes trigger regional shutdowns

outlook_24_72h.base_case: Conflict will intensify over the next 24-72h with sustained US-Israeli strikes on Iranian targets and continued Iranian retaliation against US bases and Israeli civilians. Trump projects 4-week campaign. European involvement (UK bases, French/British/German defensive measures) signals broadening coalition. Iran will likely continue targeting US assets in Iraq, Kuwait, and Gulf states while launching ballistic missiles at Israel. Risk of further expansion to additional Gulf states.
escalation_risks: Iran attempts to close Strait of Hormuz — would trigger massive global energy crisis and likely US military response
Additional Gulf states drawn into conflict — Bahrain (Fifth Fleet), UAE already targeted
Iranian attacks on Israeli civilian population centers escalating — Jerusalem cabinet meeting targeted
Russian/Chinese intervention orproxy support for Iran
Nuclear site strikes could trigger WMD escalation scenario
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: De-escalation appears unlikely in near-term. Iran has stated it will determine timing/end of war, not external powers. US has vowed to continue until 'objectives achieved.' However, if Iran suffers sufficient military degradation and faces complete international isolation (Gulf states closing embassies, European coalition formalized), Tehran may seek back-channel negotiations. Trump previously suggested rapid 4-week operation — if major Iranian military capabilities are degraded, pressure for diplomatic off-ramp may emerge by end of month.
